ISTools: Infectious Spatiotemporal Transcriptomics

Source: R/ISTools-package.R
ISTools-package.Rd

A comprehensive toolkit for quality control, analysis, and exploration of spatial transcriptomics data. 'ISTools' aims to enable users to identify spatial niches, interpret cell-cell communication contexts, and integrate diverse types of spatial omics measurements (e.g., Visium, Slide-seq, MERFISH).

The package provides a unified workflow extending the Seurat ecosystem, featuring:

  • Niche Detection: Robust algorithms to define spatial microenvironments based on cellular composition.

  • Spatial Aggregation: Metrics to quantify cell clustering and spatial organization.

  • Contextual Interaction: Enhanced ligand-receptor analysis incorporating spatial distance constraints.

  • Advanced Visualization: Specialized plotting functions for spatial maps, niche heatmaps, and interaction networks.

See Yulong Qin et al. (Year) doi:10.xxxx/xxxxx for methodological details on niche detection,

Package options

ISTools uses the following options to configure behaviour:

ISTools.memsafe

Global option to call gc() after memory-intensive operations (e.g., large matrix permutations). This helps clean up the R session memory and prevents swap space usage. Defaults to TRUE. Setting to FALSE can speed up computation in high-RAM environments.

\codeISTools.parallel.threads

Controls the default number of threads for parallel operations (using foreach and parallel). Defaults to half of the available physical cores. Set to 1 to disable parallelization.

ISTools.warn.seurat.v5

Show warning if the input Seurat object is v5 format and specific v4-compatible layers are missing.

ISTools.check.dots

For functions that have ... as a parameter, this controls the behavior when an argument isn't used. Can be one of "warn", "stop", or "silent". Defaults to "warn".

ISTools.msg.nichenet

Show message about using cached NicheNet networks to speed up initialization.

ISTools.msg.cellchat

Show message about the version of CellChatDB being loaded.

References

If you use ISTools in your publication, please cite:

  • YulongQin, et al. "ISTools: A framework for spatial niche analysis." Journal Name, Year. DOI: 10.xxxx/xxxxx

  • Stuart T, Butler A, et al. (2019) "Comprehensive Integration of Single-Cell Data." Cell. doi:10.1016/j.cell.2019.05.031 (For Seurat foundation)
